signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry
signals.text: Middle East escalation drives inflation surge, pushing Fed rate cuts to late 2026 — Reuters poll. This fuels safe-haven demand, but JPY's role is complicated by 450bp Fed-BoJ carry differential. USD/JPY at 159.43; 160.00 is the key level that has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings. Intervention risk rises on a sustained break above 160, but for now, carry advantage supports USD. Watch for a shift in BoJ bias or Fed rate expectations.
evidence: Reuters poll: Fed rate cuts pushed to late 2026

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol + Retail
signals.text: AUD/USD COT LF net long +41,675 (week ending Apr 14) with long% at 72.0%. However, retail data shows 89% short (Myfxbook), diverging from institutional positioning. HV30 at 7.8%, ATM IV at 9.1% — options pricing a 1.3 vol-pt premium over realized. Price at 0.7156; late-trend caution signal as market hedging rather than chasing. A LF delta turning positive or retail capitulation would invalidate the caution read.

signals.title: EUR/USD — CB + Technical
signals.text: ECB 2% vs Fed 3.75% — 175bp EUR yield disadvantage. EUR/USD at 1.1712; price holding above 1.1700. Positioning neutral (COT LF net short -72, long% 50.0%). ATM IV 8.5% near HV; 25d RR flat — options neutral. Next resistance at 1.1750; 1.1650 is the support.

signals.title: GBP/JPY — Carry + Technical
signals.text: BoE 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75% — +300bp GBP carry premium. GBP/JPY at 215.169; testing 215.00. Risk-off JPY bid competes with carry advantage. A sustained break below 214.50 would signal a shift in risk sentiment.

signals.title: NZD/USD — Positioning
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -17,090 (week ending Apr 14) with long% at 21.7%. Price at 0.5901; near-term support at 0.5850. Positioning aligned with price action.
